title: Halam Lancaster ED823 Memorial
cause_of_loss: Aircraft flew into high-tension power cables
narrative: A training tragedy. The Lancaster was brand new — only 12 hours on the airframe, engines installed just two weeks prior. The crew were partway through their Heavy Conversion Unit course, learning to fly four-engine bombers. Taking off from Winthorpe for a night navigation exercise, the aircraft barely made it 8 miles before crashing. Sqn Ldr W.H. Orchard's investigation found the aircraft "struck the ground flat and on an even keel with all engines under power" — but the most likely cause was contact with high-tension cables. The crew was truly multinational: two Australians, one Canadian, and four British, aged 20 to 34. Three Commonwealth airmen were buried together at Newark Cemetery; the others returned to their home towns.
erected_by: Andrew Paris and Tony Denyer (local residents)
memorial_details: Local residents Andrew Paris and Tony Denyer campaigned for recognition of the crash site. A stone memorial bearing all seven names was dedicated on 10 April 2011 (68th anniversary). Over 200 people attended, including relatives from Australia and Canada, plus representatives of the RAF, RAAF, and RCAF. The site now includes international tributes and a Nottinghamshire County Council information board.

maintainer.notes: Memorial erected by local residents Andrew Paris and Tony Denyer. Parish council area.

external_narratives.summary: Nottinghamshire County Council Roll of Honour entry for the Halam Air Crash memorial. Records the memorial to the seven crew of Lancaster ED823 from 1661 HCU who died on 10 April 1943.
external_narratives.key_details: Memorial reference 1972. Multinational crew: two Australians, one Canadian, four British. Crash site now part of Norwood Park Golf Course.

external_narratives.summary: Imperial War Museum register entry for the Lancaster ED823 memorial at Halam. Stone memorial bearing all seven names was dedicated on 10 April 2011 (68th anniversary). Over 200 people attended including relatives from Australia and Canada.
external_narratives.key_details: Memorial reference 68377. Dedicated 10 April 2011. Attended by 200+ people including RAAF, RCAF representatives. Erected by local residents Andrew Paris and Tony Denyer.

external_narratives.summary: Halam village's own account of the Lancaster ED823 crash. The aircraft was brand new with only 12 hours on the airframe. Investigation by Sqn Ldr Orchard found it struck the ground flat and level with all engines under power, suggesting contact with high-tension cables.
external_narratives.key_details: Aircraft had only 12 hours on the airframe. Engines installed just two weeks before the crash. Investigation found aircraft struck ground flat with all engines under power. High-tension cables the most likely cause.
